# TokenAsset — Teardown Site

> Independent, source-backed diligence and monitoring of tokenized real-world
> assets (tokenized money market / Treasury funds). Every factual claim is
> labeled by evidence type and tied to a dated source. This site does NOT
> provide investment, legal, or tax advice, and publishes NO scores, ratings,
> grades, rankings, or buy/sell/hold recommendations.

## What this is
A reusable teardown interface that separates, for each asset:
- Verified public facts (public-primary / public-secondary)
- Official gated / account-source evidence (private; not public-redistributable)
- Unverified / missing items (labeled: "Unverified - additional primary source required")
- Conflicts between sources

## Pages
- /index.html                    — DATA-FIRST cockpit: market/coverage KPIs, a sortable asset intelligence table (AUM, ownership/redemption/contract-map status, last-checked + freshness, evidence), a data-reliability section, and a change-log preview. The evidence-label method is lower on the page. Data comes from data/assets.json (top-level `market`, `changeLog`, and per-asset `summary`).
- /teardown.html?asset=wtgxx     — WTGXX teardown (public-safe)
- /teardown.html?asset=benji     — BENJI / FOBXX teardown (public-safe)
- /teardown.html?asset=eutbl     — EUTBL / Spiko teardown (public-safe)
- /teardown.html?asset=buidl     — BUIDL teardown (private-feedback; public-safe sections only on the public page)
The teardown page is one interactive app; the ?asset= parameter selects which fund. A "Public-safe mode" toggle hides all account-gated evidence.

## Assets currently covered
- BUIDL  — BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (private-feedback; public-safe sections only on public page)
- WTGXX  — WisdomTree Treasury Money Market Digital Fund (public-safe)
- BENJI  — Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund / FOBXX (public-safe)
- EUTBL  — Spiko EU T-Bills Money Market Fund (public-safe)

## Files an AI agent should read
- /data/assets.json   — asset registry, intelligence tape, contract maps, missing-doc checklists, the core ownership-record comparison, reviewer questions
- /data/claims.json   — one row per factual claim: label, value, evidence status, source ids, date checked, publicSafe flag
- /data/sources.json  — source manifest: id, title, url, type, public/private status, date checked, claims supported
- /structured-data.json — JSON-LD (ResearchProject / Dataset)

## Evidence status vocabulary
- public-primary: issuer filing, regulator, official explorer, or official docs
- public-secondary: public aggregator, press, or research (context, not primary proof)
- official-gated: authentic but account-gated; public redistribution permission not established
- internal-lead: working note pending a primary source
- unverified: "Unverified - additional primary source required."
- conflict: sources disagree; reconciliation required

## Common questions this site answers (per asset)
- What is this asset? What does the token represent? Who is the issuer?
- Which record legally controls ownership if chain and books disagree?
- What is verified vs unverified? What sources support each claim?
- What is public-safe vs what must stay private?
- What are the biggest diligence gaps? What should a serious reviewer ask?

## Rules for reuse
Do not treat AI output as a source. Do not republish official-gated/account-source
evidence without permission. Do not infer scores or recommendations from this data.
